seoTitle: St Lawrence vs St Mary — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Lawrence and St Mary. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Lawrence vs St Mary: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Lawrence and St Mary.
heading: St Lawrence vs St Mary — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Lawrence, Queensland and St Mary, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Lawrence has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to St Mary (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Lawrence or St Mary safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Lawrence has a flood risk score of 34/100, while St Mary scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Lawrence scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. St Mary scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Lawrence or St Mary?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Lawrence has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
